title: Electronic Writ-of-Payment Proceedings (EPU) — A Practical Guide
description: How does the e-court and EPU system work? A practical guide to electronic writ-of-payment proceedings for creditors pursuing claims.
content:
What Are Electronic Writ-of-Payment Proceedings?

Electronic Writ-of-Payment Proceedings (EPU), commonly known as the e-court, is a simplified procedure for pursuing monetary claims online. The entire process — from filing the claim to obtaining an enforcement title — takes place electronically, which significantly reduces time and costs compared to traditional court proceedings.

When Can EPU Be Used?

EPU is available to creditors pursuing monetary claims whose due date does not exceed 3 years from the date of filing the claim. The system is suitable for cases where:

  • The claimed amount is defined and its value is not disputed.
  • Documentation confirming the existence of the obligation exists (contract, invoice, payment demand).
  • The debtor does not contest the debt — or the creditor expects no objection to be filed.

EPU is particularly popular among debt collection companies, telecommunications operators, utility providers, and banks.

How to File a Claim Through the E-Court?

  1. Account registration — Register at the e-court portal or log in using a trusted profile (ePUAP).
  2. Complete the claim form — Enter the creditor's and debtor's details, the amount of the claim, legal basis, and description of the case. Physical documents are not attached — a description is sufficient.
  3. Court fee — The fee is 1/4 of the standard court fee (min. PLN 30), payable electronically.
  4. File the claim — After verification, the application is transferred to the District Court Lublin-Zachód in Lublin, which handles all EPU cases.

Payment Order — What Happens Next?

The court issues a payment order in writ-of-payment proceedings at a closed session, without summoning the defendant. The debtor receives the order by post and has 14 days to file an objection.

If the debtor does not file an objection, the order becomes final. The creditor may apply for an enforcement clause and then direct the order to the bailiff — for example, to Bailiff's Office No. IX in Otwock.

If the debtor files an objection, the case is transferred to the court having jurisdiction over the defendant's place of residence and proceeds under ordinary proceedings.

Bailiff ID in EPU — Number 2721

When directing an enforcement application to Bailiff's Office No. IX in Otwock, the bailiff identifier: 2721 must be indicated. Thanks to the office's integration with the EPU system, enforcement applications can be submitted electronically directly through the platform, without the need to print documents.

Benefits of EPU for Creditors

  • Low cost — court fee is 1/4 of the standard rate.
  • Speed — payment order issued within days.
  • Convenience — entire proceedings conducted online, 24/7.
  • Simplicity — no requirement to attach physical documents to the case file.

Need Help?

Bailiff's Office No. IX in Otwock conducts enforcement based on payment orders obtained through EPU. We are fully integrated with the electronic system, enabling a smooth, paperless process — from filing the enforcement application to the settlement of recovered funds. We invite you to contact us.
author: Harvey Specter
metaTitle: EPU — Electronic Writ-of-Payment Proceedings. Practical Guide | Bailiff Otwock
metaDescription: How does the e-court (EPU) work? Step by step from filing an electronic claim to obtaining an enforcement title and initiating bailiff enforcement in Otwock.
